reviews.review_description:
I recently tried out the demo for Rooms of Realities with a friend and I have to say, you should stop reading, gather your friends and play the demo together! It's that good! It was an incredible experience that completely changed how I thought about playing games with friends who are far away. Made specifically for Virtual Reality, this immersive escape room game not only brought me closer to my long-distance friend, but it also gave us a fantastic way to work together, solve puzzles, and enjoy quality time.

From the moment we jumped into the virtual world, we were blown away by how beautiful everything looked and how well the game worked. There was a tutorial at the beginning that helped us get the hang of things, and then we soon found ourselves in a room filled with all sorts of puzzles. Some were simple, while others really made us think, and we had to work as a team to figure them out. We had to talk a lot and help each other along the way.

One of the best things about Rooms of Realities was that we could see each other's avatars. It was like seeing our friends' faces and expressions right there with us, even though we were far apart. It felt so real and made our conversations much more fun. We could also talk to each other using voice chat, which added to the immersive experience.

Apart from the puzzles, the game had some fun moments too. We could mess around with different items in the rooms and have a good laugh together. It wasn't all about solving puzzles; we could just enjoy ourselves and have fun.

One aspect that got us excited about the game's replayability is the potential for branching levels or separating our group. Imagine if we could take different paths within a room or have puzzles that require different people to solve them, so we can switch roles on subsequent playthroughs. This would add a fresh and exciting dynamic each time we revisit the game, allowing us to explore new challenges and approaches. It's something we hope the developers consider for the full release, as it would undoubtedly enhance the game's longevity and keep players engaged in the long run.

Overall, Rooms of Realities is an amazing virtual reality game that brings friends together in a whole new way. It's great for working as a team, solving challenging puzzles, and feeling like you're really with your friends through the avatars. If you're looking for an immersive and socially connected gaming experience, you have to try this game. I can't wait for the full release and more levels to play!

developer_response.body: Woah, thanks for such a lengthy review - we really appreciate this form of constructive feedback!

reviews.review_description: Puzzles are nice, things seem to connect well. Looking forward to the whole game

We had a great time working our way through the demo, the pyramid room was very polished and an immersive experience. Judging from this first playthrough this one will only get better, looking forward to playing more Rooms of Realities.
Also multiplayer worked flawlessly with both of us on the same Oculus account.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your feedback, we are humbled by the reception <3 Also, it's great to hear the multiplayer worked for you so well!

reviews.review_description: The title is a joke, no one can top The Room. But Realities might come close. Not as atmospheric but the same types of puzzles, which are my favorite! This demo is a nice little taste of one whole room, and I'm truly excited to see the whole game. This is a great start, more more more like this!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your feedback! Hope you enjoy the full release as much as the demo <3

reviews.review_description:
Outside of VR arcades there really isn’t any option to play a multiplayer escape room and this fills a huge gap. 

I played through the demo three times will different groups including kids aged 11,12 who barely needed a hint so it’s perfect for families with multiple headsets. The puzzles are nicely varied and perfectly weighted - not too taxing but satisfying.

Gamedust are the studio behind the excellent Yupitergrad games so I’m not surprised at the quality. My goddaughter is imploring me to wait and play the full release with her!

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review! It is so cute that our little game may connect you with your goddaughter - we will try to make the co-op as fun as possible!

reviews.review_description: I'm really looking forward to the full game. I've played most of the puzzle/escape games out there and I can honestly say that this one is up there with the best of them.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review, we hope not to disappoint - especially given your high praise :D

reviews.review_title: Family and friends - you've got a winner
reviews.review_description:
Rooms of realities is a beautifully crafted game that successfully blends fun and challenges. While being an experience that is better played with friends, it can also be played by yourself.

There's only one level to the demo, but it's enough to sell you on the concept. I personally played it with my son and my girlfriend.
The biggest surprise was the immersive game gathering us 3 together in what felt like a real life binding experience. We all have very different tastes in games. I prefer survival shooters, my girlfriend is into horror or Arcady games, my son is into sandbox. Despite all playing different games, that one pulled all the family into the same world.

The puzzles are not too hard and the game gives you hints if you ask for it. You won't get lost without a queue of what to do. It is not holding your hand and there are still challenging puzzles. But the option to get a hint is always there, right on your controller.

The full game will definitely deliver more places to explore, more puzzles to solve and more great times with your loved ones.

The one negative point I would have to say, is that everything is carefully crafted to be enjoyable. While it's very positive on your first playthrough, it also means there's no real randomization. So replayability may not be a thing. But the content should be enough to go for hours or great escape rooms.

Get the demo, it's amazing and free.

developer_response.body:
We are winners?! Thank you so much <3
Your review is a great source of feedback when it comes to improving the quality of the demo moving forward to the full release. We will have to see how to involve replayability features later down the line for sure :)

reviews.review_description: I did enjoy the demo. I played with a group of friends. It was a bit confusing for us though. I hope in the final version we have better hints and hopefully a saved progress. Like for example if we in a group or solo get stuck or want to take a break. I'm not sure if that was an option. Instead of playing through to finish so you don't have to start over again. It felt like the VR version of Escape Simulator on steam. And I enjoy that puzzle game as well. We just need more puzzles🤗🤗🤗

developer_response.body:
Hi there!
Thanks a lot for your review and feedback - we will for sure will be looking at ways to make the puzzles less confusing with more straightforward hints to help new players out! Also saving progress - noted :)

reviews.review_description:
I played with a friend a the experience was awesome and we definerly need more of this!

My only complain is that there are many little bugs, specially bad hand interactions(Grabbing the wheel that spin the circle feel horrible and some grabs like the cup grab are broken).
Also didn't understand why the only cassette started with "2" if we didn't played the level 1.

Anyway, great concept, polish it, add more and it will be awesome!

developer_response.body:
Hi there - thanks a lot for your review Adventurer!
We will be for sure polishing it (as a Polish studio, it's a must :D /j) 
Your feedback concerning the bugs is very valuable to us, we will try to investigate the issues and improve things in the full release!

reviews.review_description: Really good escape room game. I wished forever for a vr coop escape room to play with friends and now it comes ❤️ im looking forward for Release 👍👍

developer_response.body: It's so nice to see your wish come true - we will try to exceed your expectations on launch <3

reviews.review_description: I love how the devs switch it up between multiplayer and single player! They made it easier in single because are alone but for multiplayer they make it a bit harder! I love the small amount of detail the devs included. 10/10 for me! Would definitely recommend to my friends!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for taking the time to write this review! We also will be focusing on making the game fresh for both solo and co-op players for sure :)

reviews.review_description:
This is exactly what is missing in the VR gaming world. There are a some puzzle/escape rooms, but none where you can go on this adventure with your friends. And what an adventure it is. 

There is a lack of coop (actual coop, not multiplayer) games for VR  and an even severe lack of coop puzzle games.

So my friends and I actually jumped for joy when we saw this demo. We thoroughly enjoyed playing this one room.  Solving puzzles, thinking about solutions together, while experiencing the whole Indian Jones Egyptian tomb atmosphere is brilliant. The puzzles aren't easy, but they are also not frustratingly difficult. The graphics are pretty good, the sound adds to the experience and the whole makes it very immersive.

We are eagerly awaiting the release of whole game.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your extensive review! We will try to build on the positive aspects of the demo you've mentioned and also add much much more on launch - so stay tuned!

reviews.review_description:
Amazing demo, play it while you can! 

A Co-op, puzzler/escape room. 
One of my favourite experiences in all my years owning a Quest headset and it’s just a demo! Can’t wait for full release…take my money

developer_response.body: Woah, that's very high praise, thank you very much Adventurer!

reviews.review_description: I've been waiting for a puzzle/escape room you can do with friends, and this one didn't disappoint.  There was enough variety to make you think and some aspects that require teamwork. Was it perfect? No, but the few bugs we ran across didn't affect game play at all.  Can't wait until the full game comes out with more levels to explore and puzzles to solve.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your honest review, your feedback will prove invaluable when it comes to finally launching the game later this year! :)

reviews.review_title: way better than i was expecting
reviews.review_description: epic, cant wait for longer and harder rooms

Our family have their own Quest 2 and enjoy playing co-op playing. 
But there are so limited titles excluding FPS which my wife dislike and always hoped a title like this one!
We all 3 person enjoyed demo together and it was very pleasant expericence without any bugging.
We hope the main title as soon as possible and more more add-ons.
Thanks.

This game just gave us the BEST time ever.  Cannot wait for the full released version.  Please update this forever so we can keep playing.

Now for some constructive feedback:  I almost wish there was a reveal on completion that confirms what happened.  For example, we sloppily rearranged the beams of light, realizing that a couple streams were unlit (i.e. not reflecting off the destinations) and we messed with them a bit more.... then all of a sudden the lights came on, noises happened (I think those were the spiked doorways coming down/opening up) and there was more to do.  But we had no idea what we did.  Not sure I have a solid suggestion on how to implement this, but perhaps a pop-up or a voiceover or some form of informational recap of what we did.  I don't think I can explain it well.  In those moments, I was happy we moved on but sad that I was left with questions.  I might just need to re-play it.  So perhaps even an option to go back and re-play a section would be nice as a group feature if the host requests a re-do. 🤷‍♀️  Yea, so I guess to consolidate my feedback a bit more then would be this.... take advantage of the digital aspects and have a re-do option available to the host, almost as if to go back and repeat 1 chapter of the puzzle.  Does that make sense?  Just to give us more awareness of what happened without having to start all the way over?  

Either way, my group and I are OBSESSED.  Even the entrance point of the game was so nostalgic with the cassettes.  Just a brilliant game.  We never wanted it to end.

developer_response.body: Hi there, thanks a lot for your extensive review - we appreciate your feedback and we are glad you got to enjoy the demo of Rooms of Realities to the fullest! When it comes to your concerns about more story-telling options of explaining some of the puzzles after completing, Bluekey Games will be working diligently to make it more satisfying to complete the puzzles, even if you do them on accident :D

reviews.review_description:
Tried the demo as I love a decent puzzle game and this does not disappoint in the slightest. 

The puzzles can be taxing but not to the point of being unfair. I played solo (never been a social gamer) but I could imagine this being a massive amount of fun with more.players. I would perhaps like some option for a hint of really stuck but that's a minor request.

Cannot fault the controls in any way. All very instinctive. Graphics and sound are very good too - No glitches for me in the demo! 

I can honestly say the full  release is definitely on my wishlist to grab when I can.

Wonderful stuff!!

developer_response.body: Your review is even more superb! Thank you so much for the kind words and extensive feedback - we will for sure be working towards the full release to build up on the good things from the Demo :) Stay tuned.

reviews.review_description: I played the demo of this game and it's an amazing game, not alot of room escape type games for the Meta Quest 2. I would recommend this game once the full release is out.
Literally had the most fun playing this with my mom last night. Team work made the dream work, for sure! Can’t wait for more rooms!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your review, we are glad you got to have some quality time with your mother in our little demo <3
